description: Build an event-driven data pipeline on Azure to ingest retail point-of-sale events from Azure Event Hubs, transform and enrich in Azure Functions, and land in Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 for Synapse Analytics consumption. Near-real-time dashboard updates via Azure Data Explorer for operational visibility.
constraints: Ingestion throughput must sustain 50,000 events/second during peak retail hours
Event ordering must be preserved per store partition key
Data lake files must be Parquet-formatted with Delta Lake transactions for ACID semantics
requiredCapabilities: Dead-letter queue for poison events with alerting
Cost controls: auto-pause Synapse SQL pool when idle > 30 minutes
assumptions: POS systems already emit events to a regional Event Hub namespace
Data science team consumes from the curated Delta Lake layer, not raw
inlineRequirements: Document replay strategy: reprocess from Event Hub capture if a transform bug corrupts the curated layer

documents.name: event-schema-samples.txt
documents.content: Fictional POS events: sale_completed, sale_voided, inventory_adjustment. Each carries storeId, timestamp, and items[].

topologyHints: Separate hot path (streaming to dashboards) from cold path (batch to Delta Lake)
Use managed identity for all data plane access — no connection strings in config
securityBaselineHints: Encrypt data lake at rest with customer-managed keys
Network isolation: Event Hubs and ADLS behind private endpoints
